subject: B727 BonJovi deal:
body:
David Burstein would like to know if you are still interested in BonJovi  
deal.
please see attachment also:
          JEGE CHARTER ANALYSIS     BON JOVI TOUR              Costs Per 
Larry / Rich Per David Difference       Fuel     65,000 gallons @ $4.00  260,000   
  59,900 gallons @ $3.25   194,675        65,325   Hotels / Meals     74 days 
@ $275 x 6 persons  122,100     Did city by city projections   77,735        
44,365   Salaries     Rental pilots only  12,000     Projected pilot costs   
134,750        (122,750)       FBO/Handling/Landing  Fees/Parking  80,431   
80,431  0       Insurance     Annual premium 265,000 x 2.50 mos  55,208     
20,000 x 2.25 months   45,000        10,208   Maintenance / Parts     800 per hour 
x 50 hours  40,000     40,000 per month x 2.25 months   90,000        (50,000) 
      Miscellaneous   16,775   -     16,775                  Total Costs  
586,514   622,591    Total Bon Jovi will pay  826,514   826,514         JEE 
Profit  240,000   203,923   (36,077)            Note:     a) David's commission 
paid  seperately from Bon Jovi therefore I believe we have room to    renegotiate 
Bon Jovi amount by him  contributing portion of commission.      




**************************************See AOL's top rated recipes 
(http://food.aol.com/top-rated-recipes?NCID=aoltop00030000000004)


From: principalair@aol.com
Subject: Re: B727
Date: Sun, 09 Dec 2007 17:13:59 GMT
To: LVJET@aol.com

Dear Larry


Greetings from London.



Furrther to our telephone conversation of 06 Dec, attached hereto please find my initial analysis and offer with respect to the Bon Jovi Tour.


The?variable costs attached show:


Fuel @ US$ 3.25 per gallon?? (This is the average of our current contract fuel with Phoenix Fuel)
Total =? US$ 194,675.

Crew Hotels =? US$ 77,735.

FBO/Ground? Handling/Landing Fees/Parking?=? US$ 80,431.

Crew Salaries, including Per Diem =? US$ 134,750.

Total Variable Costs =? US$ 487,591.

Owner Costs (FIXED):

Aircraft:? US$ 50,000. per month X 2.25 months =? US$ 112,500.
Insurance:?????20,000. per month X 2.25 months =????????? 45,000.
Maintenance/Parts:??40,000. per month X 2.25 months =?90,000.
(based on 24 month maintenance interval)
Total Fixed:?? US$ 247,000.


Total:?? US$ 734,591. plus margin at 12.5 percent or US$ 91,923.

Grand Total:? US$ 826,514.



IF,?(and its a big IF)?Bon Jovi Tours was willing to accept this figure, your yield, on a per hour basis would be

US$ 16,530. per block hour.


Excluded in the costs above are:

1- The costs to bridge the aircraft to part 125

2- All S, G, & A costs, i.e. your admin costs such as your offices, hangar,?training, Jeppesens, your
????staff salaries, crew uniforms, aircraft supplies and? commissaries, shipping, communications, etc etc

3-? Ferry flight in/out of tour; this would be charged to the customer at direct costs

4-??De-Ice, Catering, F.E.T. and other taxes, Nav Canada Fees, Immigration/Custom/Agriculture
?????Fees, and fuel escalator, i.e. costs of fuel above US$ 3.25 per gallon. ALL OF WHICH WOULD
???? BE BILLED TO THE CUSTOMER AT COSTS.

5-? P.F.C.s (passenger facility charges) and other airport taxes, again, we would bill the customer
???? directly for these charges.


IF Jeffrey has any interest in this PROSPECTIVE offer, I would like to speak with him or you ASAP as
The Bon Jovi Tour must make a decision on or before 15 Dec on the aircraft needs as their tour leaves for Japan on the 16th of Dec, and they will be overseas until they come back for the US tour in
beginning March.

Therefore, I would greatly appreciate a call ASAP regarding this matter.

I can be reached in London at? +1 215 715 1017

Many thanks in advance for your attention to this matter.


Kind Regards,


David


-----Original Message-----
From: LVJET@aol.com
To: PrincipalAir@aol.com
Sent: Mon, 19 Nov 2007 5:52 pm
Subject: B727



Hello David,

Jeffrey would like to know what time of money is expected to be paid?for Bon Jovi concert tour?

He like to see numbers as you well know.

thx,

Larry Visoski

fwd your cell number, I only can locate your email.

?

body_html:
David Burstein would like to know if you are still interested in BonJovi deal.
please see attachment also:
JEGE CHARTER ANALYSIS
BON JOVI TOUR
Costs Per Larry / Rich Per David Difference
Fuel
65,000 gallons @ $4.00                      260,000
59,900 gallons @ $3.25                    194,675
             65,325
Hotels / Meals
74 days @ $275 x 6 persons                      122,100
Did city by city projections                      77,735
             44,365
Salaries
Rental pilots only                        12,000  
Projected pilot costs                    134,750  
          (122,750)
FBO/Handling/Landing Fees/Parking                        80,431                      80,431 0
Insurance
Annual premium 265,000 x 2.50 mos                        55,208
20,000 x 2.25 months                      45,000
             10,208
Maintenance / Parts
800 per hour x 50 hours                        40,000
40,000 per month x 2.25 months                      90,000
            (50,000)
Miscellaneous                         16,775                             -                16,775
Total Costs                      586,514                    622,591
Total Bon Jovi will pay                      826,514                    826,514  
JEE Profit                      240,000                    203,923             (36,077)
 
Note:
 a) David's commission paid seperately from Bon Jovi therefore I believe we have room to  
 renegotiate Bon Jovi amount by him contributing portion of commission.

From:principalair@aol.com
Subject:Re: B727
Date:Sun, 09 Dec 2007 17:13:59 GMT
To:LVJET@aol.com

Dear Larry


Greetings from London.



Furrther to our telephone conversation of 06 Dec, attached hereto please find my initial analysis and offer with respect to the Bon Jovi Tour.


The variable costs attached show:


Fuel @ US$ 3.25 per gallon   (This is the average of our current contract fuel with Phoenix Fuel)
Total =  US$ 194,675.

Crew Hotels =  US$ 77,735.

FBO/Ground  Handling/Landing Fees/Parking =  US$ 80,431.

Crew Salaries, including Per Diem =  US$ 134,750.

Total Variable Costs =  US$ 487,591.

Owner Costs (FIXED):

Aircraft:  US$ 50,000. per month X 2.25 months =  US$ 112,500.
Insurance:     20,000. per month X 2.25 months =          45,000.
Maintenance/Parts:  40,000. per month X 2.25 months = 90,000.
(based on 24 month maintenance interval)
Total Fixed:   US$ 247,000.


Total:   US$ 734,591. plus margin at 12.5 percent or US$ 91,923.

Grand Total:  US$ 826,514.



IF, (and its a big IF) Bon Jovi Tours was willing to accept this figure, your yield, on a per hour basis would be

US$ 16,530. per block hour.


Excluded in the costs above are:

1- The costs to bridge the aircraft to part 125

2- All S, G, & A costs, i.e. your admin costs such as your offices, hangar, training, Jeppesens, your
    staff salaries, crew uniforms, aircraft supplies and  commissaries, shipping, communications, etc etc

3-  Ferry flight in/out of tour; this would be charged to the customer at direct costs

4-  De-Ice, Catering, F.E.T. and other taxes, Nav Canada Fees, Immigration/Custom/Agriculture
     Fees, and fuel escalator, i.e. costs of fuel above US$ 3.25 per gallon. ALL OF WHICH WOULD
     BE BILLED TO THE CUSTOMER AT COSTS.

5-  P.F.C.s (passenger facility charges) and other airport taxes, again, we would bill the customer
     directly for these charges.


IF Jeffrey has any interest in this PROSPECTIVE offer, I would like to speak with him or you ASAP as
The Bon Jovi Tour must make a decision on or before 15 Dec on the aircraft needs as their tour leaves for Japan on the 16th of Dec, and they will be overseas until they come back for the US tour in
beginning March.

Therefore, I would greatly appreciate a call ASAP regarding this matter.

I can be reached in London at  +1 215 715 1017

Many thanks in advance for your attention to this matter.


Kind Regards,


David
